name: code-sessions
description: Central sidebar for coding-agent CLI activity: Claude Code + Grok Build sessions across projects (with tokens/cost/subagents/projects-touched), KB file changes by day, and per-project file changes by day. Jump to resume, file, or diff.

code-planning.name: Projects
code-planning.contextualTitle: Projects & catalog entries with their ideas/plans

code-activity.name: Tasks
code-activity.contextualTitle: Tasks (Claude sub-agents, workflows, scheduled routines, crontab)

code-activity.name: Memory
code-activity.contextualTitle: Agent memory entries across CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / MEMORY.md / ~/.claude / ~/.codex / MCP memory sources

commands.title: Enable capture (install hooks + skills + start daemon)
Sync stores now (pull KB / Sessions / Planning)
Open sessions × topics graph (from CS library)

view/title.when: view == codePlanningToday || view == codePlanningInbox || view == codePlanningProjects
view == codePlanningToday || view == codePlanningInbox || view == codePlanningProjects
view == codePlanningToday || view == codePlanningInbox || view == codePlanningProjects

view/item/context.when: view == codeSessions && (viewItem == session || viewItem == sessionAutomated || viewItem == session-starred || viewItem == sessionAutomated-starred)

view == codeSessions && (viewItem == session || viewItem == sessionAutomated || viewItem == session-starred || viewItem == sessionAutomated-starred || viewItem == session-hidden || viewItem == sessionAutomated-hidden || viewItem == session-starred-hidden || viewItem == sessionAutomated-starred-hidden)
view == codeSessions && (viewItem == session || viewItem == sessionAutomated || viewItem == session-starred || viewItem == sessionAutomated-starred)
view == codeSessions && (viewItem == session-hidden || viewItem == sessionAutomated-hidden || viewItem == session-starred-hidden || viewItem == sessionAutomated-starred-hidden)

codeSessions.planning.cliPath.description: Path to the knowledge-planning CLI entry (src/cli/index.ts). Empty → ~/projects/unpolarize/knowledge-planning/src/cli/index.ts.

codeSessions.planning.nodePath.description: Node binary used to run the knowledge-planning CLI (needs Node ≥22 with TypeScript stripping).

codeSessions.planning.storeRoot.description: Planning store root (KP_ROOT). Empty → the CLI default (~/docs/planning).

codeSessions.limit.description: Maximum number of most-recent sessions to load.

codeSessions.refresh.forceRecent.description: When the Refresh button is pressed, force a full re-parse of the N most recently-modified JSONLs (regardless of mtime cache). Catches on-disk edits — most notably session renames — that don't always bump mtime. Set to 0 to use only the cheap incremental sync.

codeSessions.scriptPath.deprecationMessage: Deprecated since 1.2.2 — the session-center.sh fallback was removed because it pointed at a developer-personal script that doesn't exist on any other user's machine (caused 'exit 127: No such file or directory' on fresh installs). Sessions are read from the SQLite cache exclusively.
codeSessions.scriptPath.description: Deprecated — no longer used. The SQLite cache is the only data source.

codeSessions.cacheEnabled.description: Use the SQLite cache. Default true — this is now the ONLY data source for the Sessions tree and the Insights view. Setting this to false will show an empty-state error in the tree (the legacy session-center.sh fallback was removed in 1.2.2 because it pointed at a developer-personal script).

codeSessions.showAutomated.description: Show automated / cron-triggered sessions (entrypoint = sdk-cli, sdk, routine, etc.). Hidden by default since they're usually scheduled jobs (Google Doc digests, KB ingest, daily reports) rather than interactive work.

codeSessions.showHidden.description: Reveal sessions that have been soft-hidden via 'Hide session'. Hidden rows reappear in their normal bucket position with a closed-eye icon. Right-click a hidden row to 'Unhide'.

codeSessions.filterByCurrentWorkspace.description: Show only sessions that originated from the current workspace's first folder (or a subfolder of it). Default on — keeps the sidebar focused on sessions you can actually resume from this window. Toggle off to see every session across every project.

codeSessions.insightsLookbackDays.description: Insights dashboard: how many days of activity to chart.

codeSessions.insightsDeepParse.description: Insights dashboard: how many most-recent sessions to parse deeply for thinking-time / tool-mix / burst-rate metrics. Higher = better stats, slower load.

codeSessions.grok.enabled.description: Discover and index Grok Build sessions from ~/.grok/sessions/ alongside Claude Code sessions.

codeSessions.git.enabled.description: Import sessions from the portable git-backed store (~/.sessions) written by the code-sessions (CS) library/agent, so sessions captured on other machines and via the CS daemon appear here.

codeSessions.git.includeLocalHost.description: Also import CS git-store sessions captured on THIS machine. Off by default because they are already indexed from their native JSONL (higher fidelity); enabling can create duplicates.

codeSessions.sync.enabled.description: While this viewer is open, keep the shared git stores fresh by pulling them — the KB (~/docs, which contains the Planning store) and the Sessions store (~/.sessions). Syncs on activation, on a poll, and when a coding turn completes. Nothing runs while the viewer is closed.

codeSessions.sync.intervalMinutes.description: Minutes between background sync passes. 0 disables the periodic poll (activation + turn-completion syncs still run).

codeSessions.sync.onTurnComplete.description: Sync when a coding turn completes (a commit lands under ~/.sessions/hosts), so a session finishing on any machine shows up here promptly.

codeSessions.sync.push.description: After rebasing onto the remote, push this machine's local commits (in ~/docs / ~/.sessions) so its sessions and planning changes flow out too. Turn off for pull-only.

codeSessions.sync.extraRepos.description: Additional absolute repo paths to include in the background sync (~ is expanded). The KB and Sessions stores are always included.

codeSessions.notifications.finished.description: Show a popup when an agent session finishes (was active, then continuously idle for notifications.finishedSeconds).

codeSessions.notifications.finishedSecon.description: How long a session must stay continuously inactive before it's reported as finished. Prevents false 'finished' popups during long tool calls, model thinking, or gaps between turns.

codeSessions.notifications.blocked.description: Show a popup when a session looks blocked/stuck (in a tool call far longer than expected).

codeSessions.notifications.stuckSeconds.description: How long a session may stay in one tool call before it's flagged as possibly blocked.

enumDescriptions: Open the session in zhirafovod.code-build-vscode (unified chat UI for any coding-agent CLI). Falls back to the native per-source extension if code-build isn't installed. Note: code-build opens a fresh conversation in the session's cwd — it cannot yet resume an arbitrary claude/grok session-id, so use 'View conversation' on the row for the original transcript.

Use the per-source native extension: anthropic.claude-code (truly resumes the session by id) for claude rows; pawelhuryn.grok-vscode-phuryn (opens the panel — pick from history) for grok rows. Falls back to the CLI in a terminal if neither is installed.
codeSessions.resumeBackend.description: Which extension to prefer when clicking 'Resume' on a session. Code Build is the unified UI but loses per-session resume fidelity; native is best for resume but splits across two extensions.

codeKbChanges.repoPath.description: Path to the git repo whose changes will be listed. Leave empty to auto-detect from the first workspace folder.

codeKbChanges.lookbackDays.description: How many days of git history to load.

codeProjectsActivity.repoPaths.description: Explicit list of project repos to watch (e.g. ['~/projects/foo', '~/work/bar']). Empty by default — auto-discovery (see below) walks ~/projects/* automatically, so most users don't need to set this.

codeProjectsActivity.autoDiscover.description: Walk ~/projects/ (depth 2) and include any git repo with commits in the lookback window.

codeProjectsActivity.lookbackDays.description: How many days of git history to load per project.

codeSessions.classify.backend.description: Where topic classification runs. `ollama` calls a local Ollama daemon (free, offline). `claude-p` invokes the Claude CLI in headless mode, using your subscription.

codeSessions.liveStatusBar.enabled.description: Show a compact 'AI Agents · N active' indicator in the status bar. Polls every 5 s while sessions are active, 30 s when idle. Hover for a per-session breakdown; click to open the Live monitor.

codeSessions.awaitingUser.notify.description: Pop a VS Code toast the first time a session enters an awaiting-user state (AskUserQuestion or ExitPlanMode). The status-bar item and Live monitor banner always highlight these; this only controls the one-shot toast.

codeSessions.costBudget.daily.description: Daily Claude-Code budget in USD. When > 0, a status-bar tile shows today's spend vs this number; it turns amber at 80% and red at 100%. Set to 0 (default) to hide the tile.

codeSessions.classify.autoOnOpen.description: When the conversation viewer opens and the session has unclassified turns, run topic classification in the background and refresh when done. Only fires when `classify.backend = ollama` to avoid burning paid `claude-p` tokens automatically.

codeSessions.classify.autoBackground.description: Run topic classification continuously in the background: at extension startup the daemon scans for any session with unclassified turns and works through them one at a time, then re-scans on every sessions-view sync tick so new turns get picked up automatically. Per-turn cache means re-runs cost nothing once classified. Default backend is Ollama; see `classify.allowAutoBackgroundClaude` to opt into claude-p auto-runs.

codeSessions.classify.allowAutoBackgroun.description: Explicit opt-in: allow `classify.autoBackground` to run even when the backend is `claude-p`. Off by default so the daemon never quietly spends subscription tokens.

codeSessions.classify.model.description: Model id passed to the chosen classification backend. For `ollama`, try `llama3.2:3b`, `qwen2.5:3b`, `gemma2:2b`. For `claude-p`, try `claude-haiku-4-5`.

codeSessions.classify.batchSize.description: How many turns to classify per backend invocation. Smaller batches are more reliable with local Ollama models, which tend to drop ids when asked to echo back 20+ items. Drop to 5 if you still see 'N turns missing in response' errors.

codeSessions.classify.claudeBin.description: Optional override for the `claude` CLI path. Only used when `classify.backend = claude-p`. Empty = use PATH.

codeSessions.embedding.preferred.description: Which embedding backend to try first for the agent graph.

codeSessions.embedding.ollamaUrl.description: Base URL for a local Ollama daemon.

codeSessions.cluster.minPts.description: DBSCAN minPts for clustering sessions on the agent graph. With small corpora (<100 sessions) 3 finds clusters that 5 misses. If DBSCAN still finds none, the extension falls back to k-means.

codeSessions.cluster.epsScale.description: DBSCAN eps as a fraction of the agent graph's axis range. Smaller = tighter clusters.

codeSessions.trajectory.driftPercentile.description: Segments above this percentile of cosine distance between consecutive turns are flagged as topic drift in the trajectory view.

codeTasks.showCrontab.description: Show the user's crontab as a section in the Tasks view. Clicking a row opens the crontab in an editor; saving installs it via `crontab <file>`.

codeTasks.subagentLookbackMin.description: Tasks view: a Claude session counts as 'active sub-tasks running' if its JSONL was modified within this many minutes AND it currently has at least one running subagent. Set to 0 to disable the section.

codeSessions.planning.syncDir.description: Folder of on-demand sync scripts (executable *.sh; sync.sh = standard). Empty = ~/docs/scripts/sync. Convention: scripts/sync/README.md in the KB.

scripts.test: npm run compile && node --expose-gc test/wasm-stmt-leak.test.js && node test/wasm-stmt-cache.test.js && node test/storeSyncGit.test.js
